reviews.review_text:
-plays first time
-spawns in random neighbourhood
-kills a few zomboids
-Finds gun with 3 bullets
-kills a nearby zomboid
-gets swarmed by sprinters in 3 seconds
-dies a brutal death
-gets mad and closes game for 10 minutes
-restarts game and map
-repeat similar situation
-dies again

-recommend? absolutely.
this game is compared to being put in a corner and have very bad things done to you. but its addicting

Who knew surviving the apocalypse could be this relaxing? Just zombies here and there waiting to say hello before munching you up until the screen says, ‘This is how you died.’

-great game! 👍

With mods, without mods. With friends, solo, on a server. On the hardest difficulty, on the easiest difficulty.

There are near-unending ways to experience Project Zomboid, yet every single one shows you a little more of why this game truly is excellent at EVERY SINGLE THING it sets out to do. An actual blueprint for apocalypse simulator developers everywhere, a true hallmark of genius at inventory management, heat management, skills, combat, maintenance, proactive management of all types. On every death, you'll dust yourself off, and know so much more. On every death, you'll mourn, sigh, and get it better. Because one day, the title screen will be wrong.

Leaving a positive review because is it genuinely good. However, do NOT buy this until it is CONFIRMED TO BE COMPLETED. I fear the length of time it is taking for the dev to finish this game is going to permanently keep it from the attention and love it deserves. Like, for 99.9% of video games I legitimately do not care how long it takes for the product to be made ethically and humanely; But you mean to tell me that THIS game is on a similar development cycle to STAR CITIZEN???? A game that raised a billion dollars and still isnt done? Mark my words, people are trying to make Zomboid clones and absorb the audience that is waiting for this game to be feature complete, and one day someone who is not this developer, is going to figure it out. This game is an incredible product, and you will enjoy it even though it isnt finished, but the amount of "one day this will be a feature, just not any time soon," will push you elsewhere.

I hate to do this but i just cant recommend this game anymore. The development of this game just takes to long. The constant bugs and game breaking updates are too much. Make a nice mods list....boom game cant load, its just not fun anymore. They took for ever with build 42 then when it was updated you could not play multiplayer or co-op. after the first 50 hours of this game it really helps to play with other ppl. Then with build 42 all the best mods from 41 are just tanked. It was a good run and through the slog of all the updates i kept it installed waiting to play with my friends. What a complete dissapointment.

Would be great if the lift is working. You go to a mall, go inside the lift and when the lift door open, all zombies in front of the door move their head to look u and then try to eat you.

Also give us solar panel and semi truck please. It's hard to move between city using the avaiable car at vanilla. Last time my save that using mods broken.

I recommend the game even at its state right now as an early access game
Devs are crybabies who cannot take criticism constructive or not, release updates at a snails pace, thus why the game has been in EA for over a decade, AND mods carry the ♥♥♥♥ out of this game.

it doesn't take 883 hours to go from zombie food to slightly longer-lasting zombie food, but it's what it took for me and I have loved every minute of it. I do wish the control scheme was kinder to people with crappy fine motor skills (ADHD, hi) but I cheat just enough to keep from ragequitting the game, to make the real chomps I take and actually deserve, worth accepting my fate. and it's always awesome. 

shoutout to the run-ending bathroom. you are my bane and my greatest love

I would like to recommend this game but with behavior of the developers to not ship patches for major bugs I can't.

The most generous explonation I can see for this is the codebase is such a mess that a simple bugfix might do more harm than good.

his game is one of the best zombie survival games, everything is so realistic that once I played it all day without sleeping at all because I was afraid that I would be eaten by zombies if I fell asleep now. Playing with friends is even more fun. Once I played with a friend together, we ran from zombies together, just ran away, decided to go into the first house we saw, when we went there there was a huge crowd of military zombies who ate us, even when a friend tried to jump out of the window, he ridiculously tripped and fell. 10/10

Almost forgot this game existed. Early Access tag is making sure this game doesn't end up being abandonware or considered taking forever to update past early access. Modders have carried this game for a decade and it says alot when I can go months and years not even remembering this game existed and is worth my time playing.

well sadly i cant recommend it and this is due to them not delivering on their promises even after 10+ years,and the updates take so so so long i will be dead by the time this game is complete....lately the devs showing some good signs but still aint enough...

game premise is really cool however i cant recommend it currently as the UI/action and animation system is so clunky and disorganized that it takes away from the gameplay as you'll spend the first couple of hours of new gameplay trying to figure out the ridiculous controls and confusing inventory systems. considering this game has been around for over a decade it doesn't make sense that this has not been improved. devs are neglecting this fundamental issue and are instead pushing out new updates that screw up mods.
good game to play with the bros, Also highly addictive

I’ve struggled to find the words to describe this game. It’s incredibly hard—probably one of the toughest games I’ve ever played, TRUE difficulty if I may be so bold. Yet, despite the challenge, it’s addictive and immersive. If only I had endless time to devote myself entirely to mastering every aspect of this game. I’m not very good at it but nearly every death feels like my own mistake and it rarely feels like some unfair BS of the game etc.  

I love this game with all my heart! I keep returning to it, and I’m glad that it continues to be updated and supported.
If Project Zomboid is the game you're looking for, Project Zomboid is a perfect Project Zomboid.

awsome game. 
i survived a month 
had the biggest base
all the canned food in rosewood
was a little underweight
ate 3 rotten icecreams
woke up 3 in the morning of illnes
died
10/10 game

Guess i played it a little bit

Good game solo and MP some say its only great multiplayer but nah thats fake tho this game needs some getting used to and learning but that wont take to long ofcourse mods can also increase play value alot

Overall good game

Overall a well built game. Would I say its fun? Sort of. If you like heavy simulation and a steep learning curve than sure. Its very challenging but rewarding. 

It truly is an apocalypse game. Theres lots of mechanics to this game that make it very realistic and I do not know many games that portray that. It feels good to learn and conquer the hurdles. You will probably fail a lot but thats how you learn in this game. I have still not made it past day 30.

The character creator is really in-depth. You give yourself positive and negative traits based on the kind of build you want. Theres mechanics like weight, nutrition, strength and fitness, and skills that make your character really unique and show progression. Its crazy how in depth it gets. And thats just scratching the surface

Its really only worth it for more serious gamers in my opinion. If you don't have much time on your hands than maybe its not worth it for you. It does take a few hours just to get used to the controls and basic stuff so keep that in mind
this game is buggy as hell, but its early access so you cant really complain that much.

After sinking about 150 hours into this game, I can say 3 things: 1. You WILL learn as you continue playing and that 2. You WILL die no matter how safe you think you are, which after these 3. You WILL have fun as you begin to experiment with new mods, level up your skills, and kill hordes of zombies at one time. There is a steep learning curve to the unique type of "realism" system that Project Zomboid offers, which you will come to love after some time. For beginners, make sure to do the tutorial and start in Rosewood as your first map, it's easy to learn the locations and it has the 2 biggest police/fire stations in any of the maps, setting you up with a lot of loot to help you. Good luck and remember: This is how you died

The genre defining zombie game which is a labor of love. Always great, steady she goes updates, and a cult following to back it up. Truly a one of a kind game to keep coming back to each release. Multiplayer just makes this game even more immersive and entertaining. Would recommend to anyone who likes zombies or survival games.
I like the game a lot, a bit stressful at times but it's fun.

Really fun zombie survival game, B42 may be unstable rn but it has a lot of potential to be even more amazing than b41,

Current bugs i as a farmer dislike

**ALL MY ANIMALS ARE FEMALE IN B42**  

other than that ive had no issues except the odd crash here n there

[i]"Project Zomboid"[/i] quite easily has the worst ratio of "attractiveness of its name/title" to "score/value of actual game" i have ever seen.

If Peter Jackson had directed the Lord of the Rings just as he did, but named it "hairy-footed, often crying hero-boys' adventure time with swords", "Project Zomboid" would still be leading in the "underwhelming name"-category.

What the **** is a [i]"Zomboid"[/i]? One of those 43 different genders ("zomb-boy")? Who came up with this abysmal title? Whoever it was - we have to thank him, because a really off-putting title is an extremely efficient filter. A filter against short-attention-span zoomers, minecraft-kiddies and generally people not autistic (blessed) enough to invest dozens of hours to get to know a video game with graphics worse than those on the info-screen of one of those japanese toilets.

[hr][/hr]

[h3]What to expect:[/h3]

A long period of getting to know the core gameplay until you're skilled enough to consistently survive the early game. Took me around 30 - 50 hours or so, but i did make the decision to not look up anything in that time that wasn't already in the tutorial - and so i ended up dying several completely avoidable deaths, such as eating the first ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mushrooms i found ([i]"haha why is that totally random fever not going down at all?" "[/i][b][i]OOOOOHHH"[/i])[/b], each time losing several hours of progress.

Note how I'm saying "skilled", not "lucky", because dying ALWAYS feels just, as it is always based on a decision you took or a reaction you missed. This is how you know you're playing an amazing game.

Saves getting deleted on death is one of the games' greatest features. There simply is no mechanism in video games more efficient in making you care about your character/survival. It might make you curse your own stupidity at times, but at the same time it makes succeeding equally satisfying.

About "cutting corners": Even if you WERE extremely skilled, you'd still have to spend a lot of time to learn which sandbox settings you absolutely love and which annoy the living hell out of you.

[hr][/hr]

[h3]Stages of gameplay[/h3]

These aren't hardcoded in, but the game always progresses in the following stages:
[olist]
[*]Early game: Find non-perishable food, weapons, backpacks etc. and a place that could potentially be turned into a base. Lots of hopping from unsafe house to unsafe house with no idea which route will be safe, only taking on single zombies and hoping that you can somehow find a good spot before your character becomes so hungry, thirsty, wounded, sick, depressed or exhausted that you die.
[*]Use that base to make carefully planned runs to important places such as libraries (skillbooks), toolshops, mechanics, gas stations (cigarettes!) etc. until you are equipped and skilled enough to take on most threats.
[*]If you actually made it this far: transitioning from scavenging to farming, fishing & animal keeping. a near endless grind of maxing out a large skilltree and building a gigantic base.
[/olist]

The game really shines in the first two phases, especially the second one where you get to decide which risks to take. 
[h3]Only having very mediocre equipment, NOT knowing the world map by heart and planning out a run to a place of interest with no idea of what you'll run into is possibly the most realistic zombie/survival experience in video games right now.[/h3]

You'll be sneaking around the dark with a duffle bag that is WAY too heavy, constantly trying to find impossible "clean" spots in-between zombie hordes that would outrun you. The way you planned has you going past countless doors you don't care about, when suddenly a huge group starts banging against it from the inside. Keep going on, betting on that door to hold for long enough and risk getting eaten, or abandon your plan and maybe even the run? Your choice will determine whether or not countless hours get erased forever...
I especially love that zombies will vaguely (!) follow your scent and noises - because it means that even when you've found what you were looking for, your return will keep you on your edge. Other games have perfectly cleared, eternally safe areas, Zomboid has unbeatable (in that stage of the game) groups of zombies waiting at that window you broke to get inside (noise) or the place you rested to catch your breath (scent) and forcing you to take unexpected de-tours, exhausted and hungry. Absolutely top-notch gameplay in that 2nd stage and everything that i looked for in a zombie survival game.

[hr][/hr]

[h3]About the importance of NOT looking things up:[/h3]

This game will have you insanely frustrated at times. You absolutely ARE going to lose saves that you put at least dozens of hours into, where you grew attached to your character, the base you built etc. 
Naturally, this will spark an interest to just have the games' world map and wiki open on a second monitor. DO NOT STEAL YOURSELF THE SATISFACTION OF PROGRESSING NATURALLY
At most i would suggest to look up the following:
[list]
[*]which traits to pick
[*]how crafting, building and animal keeping work
[*]how to hotwire cars and change seats once inside (important to dodge zombies)
[*]a short, general "how-to-start-guide" (no specific world map info!)
[*]gameplay elements you genuinely don't understand
[/list]

Extremely motivating: In magazines, leaflets, trash bins etc. you'll find info on places you haven't been to and they get added to your map => [b][u]DO NOT CHEAT, WAY MORE FUN TO FIND PLACES THIS WAY.[/u][/b]

[hr][/hr]

[h3]Annoying things:[/h3]

[list]
[*]Endgame skills are bloated and not balanced. Something utterly pointless such as "knapping" or "pottery" gets its own 10-score-skilltree that one might easily ignore forever, while "carpentry" is maxed out super fast and contains both dismantling everything you find and building like 50% of all recipes.
[*]With some recipes it's hard to tell why you can't craft it. For instance a large foundry requires you to slaughter pigs and turn their leather into extra large bellows, as regular bellows that you find don't work? This is just weird.
[*]Certain skills absolutely require items (car magazines for repair), but finding those is based on RNG => can be VERY frustrating and lead to (a habit of) cheating ("where is that next car mechanic").
[/list]

[hr][/hr]

[h3]About the games development:[/h3]

The game has been in [b][u]"Early Access" since 2013[/u][/b] or so, when some of the people that might read this review were not even ****ing born. Development is still highly active and hasn't been compromised (still made by people that actually love the game, not DLC-spamming profiteurs), but it is now clear that this will never become a "finished" game. 
[b][u]This game has stable and unstable versions and you better choose wisely which one you want to invest hundreds of hours into[/u][/b]. 

Stable = safe release, but potentially years old.
Unstable = unsafe, but all features.

I lost an insanely fun ~110h run due to an "unstable" glitch i couldn't fix, but this game is so ****ing pure (made by true autists) that i still highly recommend it. 
[h3]Let that sink in: i lost a ~110 hour run due to a stupid glitch and i'm definitely, 100% giving a thumbs up.[/h3]

Oh and replace the soundtrack with eerie synth ambience, it instantly improves everything.

Zomb-Boy out.

overall peak game

you eva beeen in Victoria? the real life inspiritation

London's chuds make it hard being nice, very peak on the misery belt that conveys
strong atmosphere, worldbuilding is interesting somewhat
combat feels to easy for most of the part, strange difficulty spikes
yo, janky yobo wants to feel, don't give a ♥♥♥♥ if i see a bug

"1918 London, Rosewood Ltd.
moral pressure to heal these 
pressure to eel these
stunlocked in an alley way you thunk they had at least the curtesy to let your life finish flashing before your eyes"

bin-like
Great game! Play it with friends and if you can't convince your friends to play, get new friends.

zomboid is in my opinion one of if not the best zombie survival game. i only got the game a few weeks before build 42 went into active development but i had followed it for a long time before that. the devs and the community have always wanted the game to be the best it could be, and they reach for that goal every day. this is a title i can't suggest passing up especially if you are a fan of zombie survival.

One of the best zombie survival games of all time. Unique playstyle, with great atmosphere. It's the kind of game you find yourself roleplaying in even if you didn't intend to, saying things like, "I need to get supplies, or I am dead" or driving down a road with a car that is stalling, and feeling your heart race at thought of being stranded on a highway full of zombies with nothing but a screwdriver. You can tell the developers involved in this decade plus development care deeply about this game, and the genre of survival in general. Well worth it. Buy you and your friends a copy, or just hop in on your own and find out how you died.

Id very well recommend this game to any people wanting a difficult or easy to play zombie apocalypse.  You can make it as easy as you want or as difficult as you want with mods that further enhance your gameplay or make it even harder.  It does lag for me time to time when having multiple mods loading at once but even low end pcs can play this (if you optimize this game properly).  It does have a steep learning curve but that can be easily made into a slope if you follow tutorials or get a grip on what the game is going to throw at you.

This is a game that I love dearly. From the game play itself to the fantastic and super talented modding community. I had never heard of this game until about 2 years ago. I played it on and off until recently where i really went all in. This is one of the most technically advanced games that I have ever played. I feel compelled to leave this comment because it has recently come to my attention that people are actually complaining about "how long this game is taking to develop" It's a full game now! Build 41 was ALSO a full game. This is a PROJECT. It's in the ♥♥♥♥♥♥' name people. Harassing a team of what? Less than 40 people have created a world that not only has a clear passage of time, but evolves through events, weather, and your own mishaps (I may have accidentally burnt down Fallas Lake grocer). You can play on the same world for 2000 hours, and actually watch things erode. Anyone harassing these developers should be ashamed in themselves. You make ONE $20 purchase, and play for 100-200+ hours, and believe you have the right to say it's not a complete game?? Not to mention the hours and hours that the aforementioned lovely modding community have poured into AMAZING projects of their own. I watched a full movie from a VHS I found running from zombies. (MaeuL MS mod) I would 100% recommend this game, and I 100% recommend buying at full price. Which is NEVER something I recommend even for games I slobber over. This team has created an amazing piece of software, and you all should give them money so they keep painting this masterful portrait of death and violence. Indie Stone devs, PLEASE don't sell to the corp overlords.
I'm super new, have not survived a full week yet, also new to PC gaming, and man, I've been looking forward to PZ,  and it lives up to my expectations.

extremely omega giga giga omega game, interesting mechanics blah blah - hyper giga basically.

MP is complete ass which is sad. Constant desync on high-performance, high-bandwidth dedicated servers, causing teleporting players, desynced player states, desynced cars causing constant car crashes and broken bones.

despite allat we still playing ts peak

Incredible zombie survival simulator with a genuine PLETHORA of tweakable options to ensure a tailored experience. Insanely addicting loop. It's whatever The Last of Us and/or Walking Dead and/or 28 Days Later and/or Dying Light you want it to be!!
Do not press the yell button. Also, the best build is to remove both your arms and become karate kick master.

Me: Picked this game up for the 4 for 3 deal. $15 was attractive and I've seen adds for this for a decade. I am only playing Rimworld right now, my wife doesn't want to play Seven Days to Die again, so I said lets try this out. 4 of us played and 1 of us had fun.

Pros for Me: 
Reminds me of SAS zombie assault when I was a kid on Miniclip 
Takes bodypart damage seriously, similar to Green Hell and Rimworld.
Allows 4 players to ride in a vehicle together to go on quests and be adventurers
Allows you to pick different characters that have pretty mid-game buffs at start (team play encouraged)

Neutral: 
Does not hold your hand or walk you through their mechanics (some crucial stuff is covered)
Map is huge to support, but Points of Interest are not impressive or unique (7 days player)
Zombies never run. This makes their movement predictable, but their locations can shock you if cocky

Negative: 
 Building was inconsequential and some houses were flooded with zombies. Pipe wrench loses that fight.
In 14 hours, I couldn't make simple tools like a long-shafted spear unless maybe I was a lumberjack?
Some of the actions were inconsistent: filling a car with gas (I got 10 zombies on me, wheres the hole?)

I wouldn't go back and play it without watching a video about how to play it, and I've never had to do that. I usually figure out games by making mistakes and struggling through, but with this game, i felt that I made no progress between deaths and I felt no motivation to get to the point where I could use these metal sheets, welding tools, and other cool, seemingly late-game stuff. I wouldn't recommend it to anyone who doesn't already like zombie death games, Top-Down controls, and using a wikipedia to function.

It is day 1 of my time in Kentucky was very enjoyable, after deciding to take the family on a vacation to Louisville, and have all hell break loose. I managed to fend off the hordes of the undead, and board myself up in a hotel room.

But the next day I get a little cocky, got a pep in my step, even after Witnessing my loved ones be devoured by the undead. I still awake with a plan, and that plan was to score a generator, little did I know that i would step on broken glass, and then proceeded to get a one way ticket to the Pearly gates, courtesy of the undead welcoming committee. 

Needless to say, I hate this game.

I honestly cannot recommend project zomboid.

I like the concept of simulating a zombie apocalypse, the freedom, using mods and surviving while trying to get a hang of the deep system in the game. But  the game suffers massively from scope creep and bad directions.

As this is an early access game I feel like I should start by talking about the atrocious developing cycle. The current build (build 41) came out like 4 years ago, since then all new develompents have happened in the unstable branch. The devs have planned a really big update that completely overhauls a lot of systems and keep increasing the scope of it (they have increased so much that they had to split the update in two) but while this happens the main branch is basically on life support, kept alive only by modders. The next build will probably come with some cool stuff, but at this rate is years away.

Build 41 is still rough around the edges but the good news is that there are a lot of mods that can improve it, some of which will be integrated in the upcoming build. You'll find yourself dying a lot, so don't be upset if that happens.

The unstable version is heading to a direction I'm not confortable with, catering toward seasoned and really experienced players while worsening the new player experience. There'll be a lot of new systems that taken alone could be a small update on their own.

Project zomboid makes realism a core principle of its design decision, favouring at times really convoluted systems for the sake of it while other times throung realism ot of the window and making really dumb design decisions, and as I find myself waiting real irl minutes while my character reads a book that will give me an xp book for a specific skill level I keep asking myself: while I understand this is considered realistic, when will it become fun?

The controls and user interface are really poor.

I don't mind the characters moving slow. But the controls and inventory interface are not intuitive.

I wanted to play a challenging game, but with the controls how they are, it's just frustrating. I tried with both a keyboard/mouse and a controller. I tried for an hour to figure it out. When I died it wasn't because the game was difficult. It was because I couldn't figure out how to navigate my inventory, maneuver through the world, and would just die because my character just sort of stood there fartin' around while I knew what I wanted my character to do, just not how.

I cannot recommend this game. And I really, really wanted to like it. I'll likely try again in future to see if I can figure it out, but I feel like after an hour of gameplay, you should have the basics down. I did the tutorial 4 times, twice for keyboard, twice for controller.

Combat is fundamentally broken. Poorly designed and unintuitive controls. Telling players this is part of the game to create a horror mindset and avoid combat is just a band aid over a shockingly bad piece of design. Don't force players to play the game you want them to play. If this is really an open world give players the ability to play however they want. What is so annoying is you can glimpse an amazing game, but making the combat controls so poor just creates a barrier that most gamers - including me - just cannot be bothered to perserve with. There is not even a way to practice combat other than to spend 10 minutes setting up and loading a new game, hoping a weapon is near, walking outside to find zombies, then fighting an dying within the space of one or two minutes. Rinse and repeat. No save game to reload back to just before combat. This sucks. And there are comments about this stretching back to 2019! Avoid.
Game of my life! Steep learning curve, but highly addictive. Works really well on Linux.

13 years, slow updates, still no NPCs in sight. Updates to Unstable keep breaking mods, modders are starting to resign/give up as they gradually become exhausted by the more or less meaningless small updates on B42 that keep breaking their mods. Who could blame them? So you're essentially forced to play an 8 year old build of this game (B41) if you aspire to go for a longer playthrough.  If they keep up their amazing pace, maybe my grandkids will be able to play the finished version.

Showed lots of promise, but I won't accept anybody telling me a 2D isometric sandbox game takes 40 years to develop. Steer clear, you will be disappointed - even more so if you actually end up enjoying the game initially, only to realise these developers are utterly incapable of balancing their workload. Useless feature creep, convoluted systems, but still no NPCs, no story, nothing. Playing this game singleplayer is literally the most depressing gaming experience I have ever had the displeasure of going through. Just don't.

Boring, you gotta crouch everywhere for the first month in the game, base building takes forever, exploration is not that eventful, the slightest noise you make you attract the horde and gotta walk for 2 days straight because the zombies dont get tired but you do. overall just boring

(TL;DR: As of 05/2026, Does a lot of what I want, in a way I don't enjoy.)

I've tried. I've given the game so many hours trying to find what will make it click with me, but every 
session, I come away feeling like I wasted my time.

Coming to this game from Cataclysm: Dark Days Ahead, a major inspiration for this game, I expected it couldn't cover all the complexity of a roguelike. However, it does do an impressive job of trying to. There's a huge amount of content here, complex crafting and building, a skill system, vehicles with replaceable and repairable parts. The issue, though, is once you get to them, the sense of excitement is already gone.

The early game is excellent. An exciting survival experience where you scrape together some kit to deal with the dangers. Sadly, that tapers off fast. Whether you find a functioning car, or just manage to escape to the countryside, the game immediately tails off into a very time-consuming grind that's made worse by the games real-time setup. Even with time sped up, actions take significant time, and the time controls have no checks or safety barriers to avoid rushing into a bad situation. This leaves you having to stare intensely at a fast-forward scene, in case some random zombie decides on a cross-country jaunt.

The fixed map, while beautiful, results in the sense of wonder and exploration fading after a few plays. Roads sadly suffer from the issue of many grid-based games, where corners are really not conducive to driving smoothly on.

The skill system is where things really struggle. While it plays similar to Cataclysm, with skill-gated recipes, the added real-time needed to do crafts means practicing skills is extremely tedious, even with increased XP rates. Melee combat skills do feel pretty solid, gaining reasonably through use. Firearms, however, feel extraordinarily slow, compounded by the limited amount of ammunition available. Even with a few levels of aiming skill, the accurate range feels like its feet rather than yards, meaning efficient use of ammo becomes a near-melee range hassle.

Crafting skills are where the game really suffers for me. On a fixed map with limited, if vast, supplies available, it feels like trying to grind out skills to get past the recipe gates is extremely wasteful in both resources, in addition to real-life time, as mentioned before. Inevitably it becomes a case of raiding warehouses for basic materials to burn away on grinding, rather than using them for the actual tasks you long to do.

While I don't recommend the game for now, I will keep checking in occasionally to see how it progresses. The modding community is -fantastic-, providing a lot of functionality that helped me get many more hours out of the game than I otherwise would have.

I have 14 hours of playtime.
3 hours were in single player after fighting to figure out how to add missing basic functionality via mods.
2 hours were in a multiplayer server, which crashed 3 times in that time.
The other NINE HOURS have been spent unsuccessfully trying to join or host various multiplayer games. Completely ridiculous.

The UI is absolute garbage on any monitor made since the year 2010, adding mods is a pain, and you REALLY need mods because the UI hates you and QOL is nonexistant.

I'm sure I would enjoy this game IF I COULD EVER ACTUALLY FREAKING PLAY IT

If it weren't for the Zombie part this would be a better version of The Sims. Spent hours reading books and cooking and doing push-ups and changing the car battery, all while neglecting these same responsibilities in real life. 10/10, would live vicariously through pixel art again.
i bought this game because i use videos of people playing it as background noise and i genuinely realized im not built for this
The learning curve is steep but once you begin to understand how combat and crafting works the game becomes so much fun.
This is the Minecraft of zombie games. Highly recommend to anybody who likes grindy games with a genuine sense of reward.

Is it punishing? Yes. Is it frustrating? Sometimes. Is it the best zombie game ever? Pretty much.
Yeah, the graphics are old school and the controls take a lot of getting used to but you can customize so much and there's a hugely active modding community so you can customize even more. Want it to just be a farming sim? Cool. Want to take on massive hordes? Cool.
Very fun, mildly frustrating at times, but its the most detailed realism zombie game Ive found. Deserves a better fanbase than it has.
Sarmıyor, oyun arayüzü yeni kullanıcı için rezalet. Detaylı olması güzel ama oyun fazlasıyla boğucu + olarak sürükleyici bir atmosfere sahip değil.
Could you guys do another sale, im trying to get my friends on the game :)  fun but hard on my own

Jogo legal e com muito potencial.  Difícil e punitivo, com bastante profundidade nas mecânicas. Tem alguns modos pre montados mas no geral é um sandbox, portanto, não tem objetivos definidos, apenas sobreviver, o que pode se tornar meio chato com o tempo, é pra quem gosta. O jogo é muito configurável e tem muitos mods disponíveis no workshop. Nunca joguei multiplayer mas deve ser bem mais interessante do que sozinho.
Great game.  Little bit of a learning curve in the beginning but after that you will be hooked.
When I start a server, I end up with an endless black screen and I can't play with friends because of that problem.

Not much to say about this game that hasn't already be said.
Start playing, get addicted. =)

It's been 13 years and it still controls like a tank.
I haven't had a single death in this game that wasn't caused by controls/hitbox problems.

The game also does the original dayZ mod thing of spawning zombies about 50 yards from you no matter where you are. Moving to forests or fields just reduces the number that spawn. and it really feels like they spawn not that you just come across them too.

Probably more fun in multi-player but I don't see the controls being improved with network lag.
game zombi yg sangat seru jika di mainkan bersama teman maupun sendiri, saran saya bermainlah bersama teman
The game is not bad at all, as it seems at first glance, there is a large choice for the creativity of your fantasies and to spend your apocalypse as you wish.

great zombie survival game if a zombie apocalypse ever happen this game is the closest to irl it very engaging it make dayz look like a walk in the park, im still fairly new to PZ and this was the game i have been looking for and i am not disappointed it
Great game but also a really advanced survival game compared to others and hard to get into for new players, but its so worth it honestly.

I absolutely love this game. Even though I'm constantly dying and starting over, it's a different experience every time. I also love that this community has so many people making mods. I'm always having a great time.

the standard gameplay definitely nerfs the player several times over in order to make the "zombie threat" thing seem realistic, so you'll have to adjust settings to match real life but otherwise its a great game. fantastic even.

Don't let the zombies touch you and everything is fine. 10/10 game, can't believe it took me so long to give it a proper chance. 

Play the tutorial, play a few lives, watch a couple youtube videos, and you'll be golden. Also mods on this game are peak.
its an ok game i like it alittle bit probably not much because i dont have anybody to play it with

peak game. a lot of mechanics you didn't even know you'd want in a zombie apocalypse game. its extremely immersive and its modding community makes the experience even better if you get bored of the base game
It is the Jack of All Trades of survival base building games.  Like the Sims meets The Long Dark, but with zombies
It's a really good game. It's hard to get started but it gets really fun

Hands down the greatest zombie survival game I've played. I haven't managed to make it past week one in any runs yet but that's besides the point. This game is so filled with small details that add so much to the realism and story telling around the map. Would definitely recommend if you want a difficult yet fun and entertaining story generator/sandbox.

Occasionally  it runs into bugs, be wary of that. But otherwise this is an outstanding game, I've had a lot of fun playing, with lots to do, and no specific method required to survive, you get lots of opportunity to find what suits you. Surviving is a challenge, but seriously a fun game, if you're used to open world, don't let the format intimidate you, the game is very playable and is so detailed it is still incredibly immersive. This game is seriously worth playing! I look forward to future updates.
After almost 1000 hours on the game i can finally confirm that there are zombies in kentucky
Pretty good game I'm into it after playing for 48 hours it's worth the 20 bucks and people who make mods are actual good scripters and modders

This is a pretty unique survival crafting indie game. Despite zombies and crafting being quite satured zomboid fills a new an interesting niche with its runescape like top down control of your character. The survival covers a lot of realistic needs and progression feels like finding real solutions to living in a post apocliptic setting. The late game does not have much to do but I really await friendly npcs and how the affect the descent into apocalyspe.

I have read the other reviews on this game before i bought it and can say that its better than reviews say. me personally think that its worth more than 20 dollars. yes, it doesn't have the graphics but man it has everything else you need for emersion. I recommend this game. I didn't realize i had put 87 min in the game already. i was enjoying myself so much and trying to survive that time flew by, and again its worth more than 20 bucks so just pay the 20. that feels like a discount. 

First house i went in i closed windows then closed curtons, found a freezer with frozen meat and turn oven on, turn dial to 400 and put my frozen ham in and waited. went to bathroom washed my kitchen knife cause i had zombie blood on it came back pulled ham out and cut into pieces and ate it. 10/10. left the house and was like wow its dark went back locked my door. next thing i hear is a pounding on my window. then glass breaks zombie climbs through and i stomp its head. go wash my hands and eat more ham lol. It has been a blast so far and looking to so many more experiences with my play throughs solo or with friends. I could see this game being so fun with friends. I need friends haha.

This game is absolutely phenomenal. I've been playing since last year and haven't been able to put the game for over a month since. Its a dream come true game that depicts the zombie apocalypses really well. My only issue with the game is that playing solo definitely gets lonely and boring at times. But if you have good friends to play with you will never get bored,

MP is hella jank, good gameplay ofc needs you to have a group to play if you can sit through some of the jank such as desync, insanely long load times and often times poor performance (♥♥♥♥ doesnt matter what pc you have) due to the volume of assets being loaded. Insanely fun time sink. Its not too hard to fight off a hoarde but at the same time if you're too relaxed about it you'll easily get overwhelmed. A simple to understand but mechanically complex game, All i can say is NEVER GO TO LOUISVILLE and the MALL. some ♥♥♥♥ straight out of the walking dead episode when they are in Atlanta.
This is absolutely my all time favorite Dystopian game maybe even favorite game in general
this game is amazing i decided to make myself look like walter white and quickly died after eating garbage candy

Super fun game. Love the crafting and skill mechanics. Can be very challenging at first but once you get past the learning curve  it isn't bad at all. Overall great game and killing the zombies is tons of fun. Only critique would be that the game needs to be optimized... for example, the default memory allocation is only 3GB. I've ran into my fair share of issues with lag and other performance issues, would be 10/10 if the devs would optimize. For now, I give it an 8/10.
Not for everyone, its a little bit slow, and, has alot of machenics. But 10/10 would recomend too anyone that has steam.
hard, but in a fun way. mods are cool, community is cool, everything is cool. also i love how there is splitscreen for controllers!
Love the game, please keep up the good work, devs. It is appreciated!

Very fun game, played on mac a few years ago and it ran smooth, and now I'm playing it on windows. Just a really good game with amazing guns(simple with not that many but each suit a specific role) and melee combat is amazing b/c of sound design
Very fun game with alot of good mods, i have a potato and i can pay it just fine

PZ hits differently because it doesn’t care about your power fantasy; it’s an unforgiving psychological trial with a brutal learning curve where your own panic, impatience!, and overconfidence kill you faster than the biggest horde. The sheer terror of character death and having to start again turns mundane tasks into high-stakes tactical operations, creating unforgettable, player-driven stories along the way – and yes, I will still risk my life just to hoard tons of useless junk I will never need. ^^

It prioritizes insane mechanical depth over fancy graphics, giving the game a unique charm and style that is genuinely great. What makes it legendary is the developers' relentless dedication, steadily evolving this masterpiece year after year since 2013.

The multiplayer can be a bit buggy at times but is an absolute blast with friends, especially when backed by a massive library of incredible mods. The only real headache is the frustrating process of setting up a dedicated server with mods, which desperately needs a smoother overhaul.

this game is like being force-fed bleach and being thrown into a corner, having massively horrible things done to you. the game has a accurate depiction of handsworth and birmingham in the UK. but it is addictive and fun, especially if you are playing as one of your real life friends or teacher (or just playing as some random guy) really fun if you make custom scenarios and make all firearms common and everything else.
Very cool game. Runs on 6 gb of DDR3 only with alternate launch
Shooting guns helps you survive longer. Strongly recommend

Started the game and ran the tutorial. Bashed my undead mother in the head with a frying pan, then fell victim of a racoon who kept gaslighting me.

Started my first real game. Started in a house with a gun. Awesome! Stepped outside and took 3 shots at a zombie. Missed every shot and I'm out of bullets. Oh well, just push the zombie down and stomp his head. Oh right... the tutorial mentioned something about using guns and the noise they make. That explains the tsunami of zombies. RIP.

Next run: I've learned a lot! I've got this! Driving down the road in my new, pristine van which has 150 storage space in the back. Everything is good with the world... hang on, did that zombie have a duffel bag? I WANT that bag! Sure, there's like 100 zombies around, but I'll just hop out and quickly bash that zombie. In and out, 20 second adventure.

Now my neck is lacerated, and I'm wondering if I'll survive the blood loss long enough to remove the bandage and stitch this up with a needle.

Oh and I didn't even manage to get the duffel bag.

10/10, would make idiotic decisions again.

My favorite game of all time. What it strives to do it accomplishes it beautifully, everything is made with intention and payoff. It's the kind of game where you go, "I wonder if this works", and I guarantee it does. Even if it does not, there is an expansive steam workshop with dedicated modders and devs that apreciate the work the community puts in, often times integrating popular modders into their own team. This game instills the perfect feeling of apocalypse. That these are end times, and that death is but certain. Though the game manages to balance this feeling of hopelessness with moments of determination, only to twist the knife with events like the helicopter or far away sounds of gunshots. Despite the player being alone, the world they are in feels full. Thus it is this feeling that makes Zomboid special, how lonely the apocalypse truly is. The only problem I have with this game is the lack of a goal. Though I'm certain future updates like build 42 will help with late game skills, not to mention the future goal to add NPCS, will help provide more dynamic and emerging stories, there still isn't really a goal other than to survive. While this is completely fine, it just makes it hard to be motivated sometimes if an arbitrary goal isn't made. But the story being told isn't intended to be one of grand proportions. After all, this is the story of how you died.
One of the best zombie games ever created the game is intentionally vague so you have room to learn all in all i would recommend

This is a hyper-realistic and difficult, zombie survival game. Where you are very likely to die quickly, There is online multiplayer and a wide variety of Workshop addons to add to your game. The game is easy to learn but very difficult to master and will punish recklessness.

The level of realism and stuff jampacked in this game is way too much. I added a cup of coffee in a copper cup in a microwave and it burned my base down. 

DO NOT PUT ANYTHING METAL IN THE MICROWAVE!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Needs a rebalance on some professions and other minor stuff, but all in all, everything that you could ever need is already in mods. Devs, add some mod stuff into the base game, especially the Common sense mods.

Love the constant fight for survival. It feels almost real. Each character is a new story. Fun maps and awesome zombie battles. If you are looking for a hardcore zombie survival simulation this is it.
